summary: EXP-149 adds a new paragraph to SEC-1.8 other-side section addressing the n(r) empirical fitting defense. Key argument: n(r) calibrated against Polaris elevations must also predict solar observations through the same medium — but it produces 52% solar diameter variation vs 0.01% observed (cross-ref Section 2.4). This is the overfitting proof: one in-sample success, immediate out-of-sample failure by a factor of 5,200. Paragraph slots after the wavelength-selectivity defense.
